title: Weather alert for elevated fire danger in Western NY, Finger Lakes until early Thursday evening
summary: National Weather Service issued a fire danger alert for 13 Western NY and Finger Lakes counties (including areas near Syracuse) effective until 5 p.m. Thursday, April 9, 2026, due to dry conditions, high winds (20-30 mph), and low humidity (25-35%). The statewide burn ban remains in effect through May 14 with no burn permits issued; residents are advised to use caution with ignition sources.

entity_refs.name: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation

political_signal.description: State-level emergency alert requiring local coordination and public communication; aligns with public safety priorities and state mandate compliance.

processing_notes: Alert is time-bound (expires early Thursday evening 2026-04-09); recommend monitoring for renewal.
Geographic scope includes 13 counties but article sourced from Syracuse.com, suggesting relevance to Onondaga County region.
New York State burn ban (state-mandate) is external regulatory driver; DEC website referenced as authoritative source.
No mechanical extraction of Syracuse-specific departments needed; SFD referenced generically for alert distribution.
